reviews.review_description:
All in all, pretty amazing bang for your buck. Would be really cool if we could :

* at the end of a stage see how many alpha charlie delta and miss hits (i know we have the color system, and penalty summary, but this is more like what we have IRL).
* in the leaderboard see HF, time and score: useful if we are comparing to friends, HF doesnt say how far/ahead we are in terms of points or time
* Add some benchmarking stages to the essentials (bill deill, 2shot reload 2shot, 3 target transitions, etc) and allow shooters to see their stats (first shot, reload speed, transition times, split times) so that we can measure and compare those specifics

Hope you find these ideas of value, I have more!

Pedro

Great training tool for people looking to improve their practical shooting skills. You don't get to really train recoil management but the real value is being able to train transitions, proper trigger press, target focus and more without firing real rounds. The fact you can move and shoot around a virtual course of fire is incredible. Great application.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words — this is exactly what Ace is built for! We're thrilled it's making a difference in your training. See you on the range!

reviews.review_description:
Excellent piece of training gear. Top skills that it helps:

1) Picking up the dot. I’m much better with my carry weapon after working with this for a month. 

2) Transitioning between targets. Way better now at the range. 

3) Tracking moving targets. Not something you get to do at many ranges. 

Keep up the good work, Ace!

Edit: 
Since the server “migration” this app has been a flaming dumpster fire. Nothing is consistent, it can’t track scores accurately, I have multiple missing days of shooting, and most scores don’t match up. 

It has made me just not want to use it. I already renewed the year subscription, otherwise, I wouldn’t purchase another month. If Ace can’t get their sh*t together before by subscription renews, I can’t see it being worth paying for anymore.

developer_response.body: We appreciate you leaving a review, though we're disappointed to hear the experience didn't meet your expectations. We would love to hear about why you weren't able to receive the training value our system is designed to provide. You can email us at support@acexr.com.

reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed this initially but since they
 updated their system that makes you have to find the login from your phone to the VR headset and typing in a code sent to my phone on the VR headset, I've drastically stopped using this. Made me realize that the range is the best place to play.

developer_response.body:
Hello,

Thank you for your feedback on this matter. We've recently updated our login flow to give you more options. You also should not need to log in every time you want to use the app. If the system is requiring this of you, please email us at support@acexr.com to help rectify the issue.

reviews.review_description: Initially I was skeptical that this would be anything other than a game to play and waste time on the VR system. Now after using daily for several weeks I went back to the range.....WOW my skills have grown exponentially and I'm amazed at how much of the game has transferred into the speed and accuracy of my shooting. I have introduced my children to the acexr and have full confidence that they will be able to protect themselves and have more confidence in the handling of firearms in edc scenarios. The amount of money I have saved in ammunition alone has paid for the membership and handset several time over. I plan on referring friends and family to the platform. I'll be a lifetime customer.

reviews.review_title: One of the best investments I’ve made
reviews.review_description: Absolutely love the ACR VR shooter app. I’ve been improving daily on basic fundamentals without having to drive to the range or spend money on ammo. The app provides stages for almost every aspect of handgun shooting and the skills you pick up in game WILL transfer over to your real firearm. They are always working on improvements within the app (sometimes there are some hiccups but the developers are quick to fix them). You can pick from many handsets as your controller  allowing you to get really comfortable with your actual firearm. Iron sights or optics, the choice is yours! You can even create groups and invite your friends to compete online (let the trash talking begin). I would highly recommend this to anyone looking for a fun way to improve your skills!

I recently moved to another state, ended up in a neighborhood and haven't been able to shoot since I moved here. At my old house I had a range set up and could shoot whenever I wanted.
I've had Ace for three and a half months, have over 34,000 rounds shot in game and saved over $10,000 in ammunition, not that I would have been able to get close to that round count so quickly in real life, but that's what Ace is all about. I averaged 500 live rounds a month before I moved, 250ish at USPSA events and 250ish at home.
Don't get me wrong, nothing else compares to live fire but Ace is dang close. 
In the short time I've had Ace they have updated it several times, I can't wait to see what's next.(I'm hoping for a 3 gun mode or at least PCC!)

reviews.review_title: I’m better in real life now. Worth it $
reviews.review_description:
My double taps are faster in real life now. This is a money saver! Initial cost is high (if you don’t already have the headset). The subscription and controller is worth every dime. 
Thank you ACE team. 
*As with anything, there are random bugs with this game. Have patients. Sometimes I have to “quit” the game or restart the headset to fix some issues. 
Seems like switching from using wifi or not during start up caused issues with the controller (gun/no gun/remote/no remote) Restarting with wifi on usually fixes it for me.

This has been a great learning tool between sessions at the range.

It’s good for basic dry fire and trigger work, but for more advanced students being able to work speed and first round accuracy out of the holster￼ along with rapid target transition is where you’re gonna get the most real mileage.

Someday, when they can figure out how to incorporate recoil it will almost be perfect. ￼

Iv been using ACE daily since I got it for Christmas last year (2025). Im pushing 250,000 rounds down range and about $70,000 in savings on ammo. something that would not have even been possible to attain without a system like ACE. My training has been so on point lately and I couldn't imagine knowing about this and not having it. I could not recommend more. I can not wait for them to come out with rifles

Wow. I was a doubter. How can a video game improve my shooting? Well it has. Training with Ace VR has had quite an effect on my performance at the range. My target acquisition is quicker. My splits are lower. My transitions are faster and smoother. I'm using it as a serious training tool and I see a big improvement. For reference, I don't play any other video games, vr or otherwise, and I shoot about 1000 rnds a month at the range.

Honestly its false advertising saying you save money and time from not wasting ammo at the gun range, without real slide (recoil) action this is nothing but a shooter like every other fps...the slightest bit of recoil changes everything when it comes to shot dynamics.  Don't waste your money and just play any shooter, this is an expensive joke that comes with a subscription

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for the feedback. We're transparent that our handsets don't simulate physical recoil — this is a limitation of the Meta Quest headset's capabilities. We focus on trigger control, draw speed, accuracy, and real-time metrics to build genuine shooting skills. We're sorry it wasn't the right fit and appreciate you sharing your experience. Feel free to reach out to us at support@acexr.com if you'd like to discuss further.

reviews.review_description:
If you are just trying to improve your game or just wanting to have some fun getting some trigger time.. no matter what you want to focus on… I find ACE to be a really useful tool, especially if you are having trouble getting to the range. 
Overall nice job from the ACE team.

reviews.review_title: A lot of fun and helpful practice
reviews.review_description: I've really enjoyed using this app to train and get faster at moving from target to target. The gun and optic options are great and they keep adding to them. The company listens to comments and requests from users. Definitely worth it.

As an avid shooter and someone who has done this for a living, Ace is an incredible training tool that allows you to work on man practical elements all at once in the comfort and convenience of your own home. I couldn't be happier with it or recommend it more.

Purchased the yearly membership thinking it would be something that can be used between range visits. Very disappointed! More game than training! Definitely not worth it when there's better working apps out there.
Purchased the membership for the year and now I'm finding out even though I only used it once or twice there's NO refunds on the membership. 
Now gotta try to get a refund from the credit card company after only having had it from 4/18 to 4/24.
Not a way to do business!

developer_response.body: We appreciate you leaving a review, though we're disappointed to hear the experience didn't meet your expectations. We would love to hear about why you weren't able to receive the training value our system is designed to provide. You can email us at support@acexr.com.

reviews.review_description: 10/10 would recommend for anyone even somewhat interested in shooting. Great for beginners to learn the basic fundamentals of shooting and firearm safety without any risk of danger and great for experienced shooters to stay on top of their game.

As a user with the CZ handset this is pretty close to the real thing. The ergonomics are spot on, and being able to use my actual grip panels makes the draw and indexing feel completely natural for muscle memory. However, I’ve noticed the weight distribution is definitely more back-heavy than my actual firearm. It would be a huge improvement to have some weight in the front as well to better mimic the feel of a loaded magazine and a steel slide. Overall still pretty solid for training draw speed and sight acquisition

Ace has been great in working on stage planing and working transistions. Dry fire has always been bouring to me and this is not only a blast but useful training. I’ve been able to talk 5 of my friends into buying it and that makes it even more fun to chase scores.

reviews.review_title: The only thing better is live fire!
reviews.review_description: Absolutely enjoy this app. I shoot almost every day. It has helped my speed and accuracy. If the gun actually bucked when pulling the trigger, it would be perfect.

This game is cool its a useful tool when you want to get some range time when you cant get to the range, but its definetly not the same. 20 dollars a month just to be able to play a game is kinda ridiculous tho which is why i canceled my membership

I spent hours to activate it, without success. I'm waiting for an answer from the developpers. I bought the full set months ago, but I wasn't able to use it, lack of time.
Thank you

developer_response.body: Hi, we're sorry to hear you've had trouble activating! Please reach out to us at support@acexr.com and we'll get you sorted right away. We want to make sure you're up and running as soon as possible!

reviews.review_description: This has been unusable for months, constant bugs, awful login process requires you to juggle your phone for an email link. It is laggy and unusable. Devs are either incapable at this point of fixing it or simply don't care to. Don't waste your money

developer_response.body: Hi, we're sorry to hear about your experience and appreciate the honest feedback. We'd love the chance to help get things working for you — please reach out to us at support@acexr.com and our team will work through the issues with you directly. We're actively working on improvements and want to make sure you're able to enjoy Ace!

reviews.review_description: I have not been able to cast to any of my TVs followed the process. It still no joy. This might not even be in Ace.

developer_response.body: Hi, we're sorry to hear about your experience! This is a connectivity issue between the Meta Quest headset and your TV. You can try casting to a laptop or mobile device first, then screen share to your TV from there. Feel free to reach out to us at support@acexr.com if you need any help getting it set up!

reviews.review_description: Love using Ace VR to practice uspsa style stages, especially when unable to get to the range!
This has improved my true to life shooting 100%! My speed and target acquisition is much higher than it was. Amazing program. Pistol feels like my real 1911. Love it

reviews.review_title: Updated login process not worth it
reviews.review_description:
Update

Server upgrade was rough stats lost things not working smoothly feels like it’s in BETA

Hardware plus monthly fee and constant relogging In ruins the experience in use it less. Make it about our experience not about picking down the app to prevent…..

I’m not sure why it’s so hard to get in



Old review


ACE XR is hands-down one of the most impressive and enjoyable games I’ve ever played. I’m a huge John Wick fan, and this is the closest I’ve ever felt to that level of realism and immersion in a virtual shooting experience. The physics are incredible, and the experience is completely immersive. Everything from movement to weapon handling feels intentional and authentic, not gimmicky.

What really sealed it for me is that I’ve spoken with people who actually teach firearms instruction at real shooting ranges, and they’ve told me ACE XR is a legit training tool, not just a game. That says a lot.

Beyond the training value, it’s also incredibly fun, relaxing, and engaging. I find myself jumping in all the time because it’s both mentally stimulating and stress-relieving. The performance tracking and trending data are excellent. I love being able to see progress over time and push myself to improve.

The constant addition of new drills and courses keeps things fresh and exciting. There’s always something new to try, which makes it easy to keep coming back without it ever feeling repetitive.

And the handset is outstanding. It’s extremely well made and feels solid, balanced, and premium in the hand. Playing on the Meta Quest 2, the tracking is excellent, and the physicality of the handset combined with the physics makes the experience feel incredibly real.

Overall, this is a really great experience and super fun. I play it all the time, recommend it to anyone even remotely interested in shooting sports or XR, and honestly think ACE XR is setting the standard for what this category should be. I truly wish there were more experiences like this on the Meta platform.

Highly, highly, recommend!

developer_response.body:
Hello, I am very sorry to hear about these issues. Our server upgrade did create some issues. The major issues should already be fixed, and we are working on the remained of issues currently.

Please ensure you are on the latest update for the best experience - Currently it is 0.13.7

Please also email us at support@acexr.com for any specific reports so that we can properly address/investigate these!

reviews.review_description: This is great for training about competition and get experience in target adquisition and red dot mechanics.

I'm not a big game player. I have tried many games and most can't hold my attention. I can only shoot so many zombies before I get bored. I have shot USPSA, SCSA, ICORE and IDPA coast to coast in past years but today I primarily shoot Steel Challenge. When this game came out I knew I had to give it a try. Ace has all 8 Steel Challenge stages and they look pretty realistic. They have went a long ways to add a lot of detail to the guns and stages to make it pretty realistic. I find it is a great tool to add to your other practice such as dry fire but helps keep the practice from being boring. It's a great tool to practice different shooting sequences on stages to see if one works better for you. Being able to switch optics and choose from many of the popular ones in use is a great feature. You can switch optics to see if one works better for you and if not just try another or switch back, all for the cost of a couple of clicks and not buying any actual hardware. I am rated as GM in several divisions and find this useful tool to help stay in practice when not able to make it to the range.

There are a lot of other drills and USPSA stages and drills to play with as well. Being able to practice shooting clamshell, swingers, Texas stars and experimenting with the timing and shot sequence on many of the common props makes this a lot of fun as well. There are some just plain silly shooting stages if you just want a diversion from your practice in the game as well.

The only thing the game does not curranty have are guns for PCC shooters and nothing for the rimfire pistols shooters that are very popular in Steel Challenge. The are some 3D printer folks selling a few options but so far nothing from Ace. I'm hoping that this changes in the future. I'm giving them a 4 star rating for now to give them room to improve to a 5.

I shoot GSSF. This game has been tremendously helpful for my dryfire training, and I have seen real world results. The monthly fee is a drop in the bucked compared to my practice ammo budget before the app, and is nothing compared to the time commitment (and fuel) of driving to and from the range. With Ace I can practice any time of day or night. I can practice on the road and in hotels. It’s awesome. I won my first Glock certificate less than six months after purchasing Ace, and have won cash awards from Glock as well. This game has more than paid for itself many times over. I cannot recommend this platform enough. I still go to the range a few times each month, but it’s mostly about confirmation now as my times improve. I’m looking forward to getting started in IDPA, ,PCSL, and USPSA in the next few weeks - once I’ve purchased all the requisite gear.

Firstly, a huge THANKS to the Ace XR team for the excellent service received.  From placing my order to receiving my kit took roughly about 10 days, and that was delivery to South Africa!. 5 stars just for that. The information and process to get started was super simple and easy. I actually got the kit before the delivery of the Quest 3. We'll done to the team. The game itself is super realistic and the weight of the gun adds to the realism. As an avid shooter I would definitely recommend this as a training aid. Most definitely the next best thing to being on the range. Looking forward to more stages and hopefully will get handsets matching my current firearms. 👌💯🌟🌟🌟

I'm a retired law enforcement firearms instructor that's done a fair bit of competitive shooting. I was hesitant to get this at first but I absolutely love it. Highly recommended not only for competitive shooters but anybody interested in getting proficient at handgun shooting.

After watching a YouTube video about just how effectively dry firing (using airsoft) can directly translate into real skill, I was sold on this idea! Saving money and time, and gaining the ability to essentially conjure up numerous dryfiring drills from the comfort of my home is quite the convenience. It's so fun to pla... practice, and the handset itself feels realistic and reliable! Hands-down worth it imo; looking forward to continue seeing results 🌿

Everything about this Ace VR system is amazing. It is so incredibly well thought out.   the experiences are incredible. The realism with the ace handsets makes the feel as though I'm using my own personal weapon.   the  practice I get every day with and has helped with  the proper hand grip,  the acquiring the Red Dot,  front sights have greatly improved since starting this.    I look forward to all the updates that they continue to do  and all the new builds that are coming out in the future.   this is amazing thank you

A great addition to your training! It’s not a substitute for dry fire, but it brings a fresh perspective that dry fire can’t quite match. Even though you don't  feel the recoil, you can still see it. Helping you develop a target focus approach. 

If you’re into competitive shooting, this can really help you strategize your approach to different stages. It lets you discover what kinds of transitions work best for you. 

Would like to see holster included with the product, especially at its price point. As well as more platform options. ￼

Ace, great job and design work! The realistic feel and view is great. I'm sure it will only get better as tech advances. But for where it is now you can get some really good practice sessions in prior to a match. And really, you can do it anywhere and anytime. Spend 10, 20, 30mins or until your battery dies and you walk away feeling like a more confident shooter. That visual side you get in this system really adds alot to your training.

I have seen lots of youtube influencers highly recommend this game. Many have reported improvements in real life applications. I was thoroughly impressed how realistic this game is. The controller exactly mimics my real world tool. I was surprised that the dot moves realistic to recoil. I haven’t had a real world chance to see if the skills translate yet as it’s still cold and snowy but I can say I attended an indoor gallery corse match and I did see Hugh improvement. I must say it has been good

reviews.review_title: Best Way To Dry Fire Train
reviews.review_description: Fun, effective, and covnenient. I’ve dry fired using other methods (laser cartridges, Mantis systems, dry fire magazines with trigger resets) but the ACE system is by far the best way to go. Helps improve target acquisition, target transitions, trigger pulls, reaction times, and stage familiarity. It really is an awesome experience.

The app is not so user friendly. There’s no instructions once in the app. 
The system is glitchy and the update has made for poor red acquisition. 

I like the idea but it has a long way to go.

developer_response.body:
Hi,
We appreciate you posting your feedback on our system. We do have a series of tutorials available on the top right corner of the main TV menu screen, as well as in the support page of our website. If there are still any questions you have, please reach out to our support team at support@acexr.com. Please also report bugs and glitches to our support team as well, so that we can forward them to our software team to be fixed.

reviews.review_description: Just got my new Meta headset and I have to say this is the funnest game ever. I absolutely love shooting regular guns, but it could super expensive on ammo. This is a great training tool I really enjoy it and the fact that I have a Taryn tactical gun when I don’t get to have one at home because I live in California super fun love it

My friend from the gun range let me try his Meta 3 and fire a few drills!  I like it.  Lately I’ve been home with kidneys stones and couldn’t get to the gun range!  I’ve been practicing every day!  
I shoot the daily challenge,  the robot zombies and wackamole, plus several other drill and thrills!   
I also like that I can access the internet!
I’m sure I’ll find a few more games to play!

I have made noticeable leaps and bounds in the first three weeks of use. If you cannot go to the range and shoot, this is the next best thing. ￼￼ hands-down money well spent. The only downside is the limited handset model options. That being said, I’m sure this is only temporary because as the company grows so will the inventory.

Well, I got a Quest specifically to train with Ace. It is pretty much the only thing I use on the headset. The build quality of the P365 handset is 10/10, great weight and controls are identical. Since November, I’ve played pretty consistently, already at 40k rounds. It has a counter and a dollars saved stat, at $0.30 cpr, it’s a nice number to know how much it would have cost to train with real ammo. The devs keep you engaged with the training, with Daily, Weekly, and special events. There are so many different drills. I am limited by space, but still have the ability to complete all of the drills with the teleport feature. They just updated the app and it looks so much cleaner and smoother, even on my basic 3S. It has been a noticeable training tool for live fire. 

The best part is I don’t have the itch to pay for a range membership or lanes for that matter, as often. Sometimes I just need to scratch the itch of shooting and I can use this whenever!

It is extremely well put together. The environments feel realistic. The sound design, the way everything responds, the overall feel — it’s clear a lot of thought went into making this as true-to-life as possible. It’s hyper realistic without being complicated or frustrating.

The handset is what really surprised me. It’s exceptionally well made. Solid, balanced, and it feels right in your hands. The tracking is consistent and accurate, and it adds a level of immersion that standard controllers just can’t match. It feels purpose-built.

Whether you’re working on fundamentals, target transitions, speed, or more advanced drills, you can tailor the experience to whatever type of marksmanship training you’re trying to accomplish. It’s not just a game — it’s a legitimate training tool.

Performance has been smooth, menus are straightforward, and EVERYTHING JUST WORKS.

As a lifetime shooter this is hands down the best training simulator I have ever worked with.  While serving in the Army and as a police officer this system would have been an invaluable asset for training.  The drills and challenges available allow a significant savings that enhance near realistic skill development made easy for daily practice.  Having the ability to practice daily with almost no prep makes it more likely to become part of a regular train routine. I look forward to daily and weekly challenges and the other future offerings.

reviews.review_title: The Reason to buy a Meta Quest 3
reviews.review_description: Over the holidays I was at a friends shop and one of the other patrons had received the meta quest bundle with mr920 handset and holster… and was telling everyone how great it was and so had the opportunity to try it.. and I was impressed by it so much that I bought it right after, I have essentially used it almost every day since I received it and the skill and muscle memory learned in Vr are translating over into real steel. Get it, use it, you will not be disappointed!

With limited use to ranges and outside weather conditions during the winter I was looking for an alternative training tool. As an LE, firearms instructor and breaking into competative shooting I use this all the time. With struggling shooters ACE is great for practicing holster draw, aquiring red dot, and transitions to other targets. Even with  busy lifestyle I’m able to jump on 15 minutes a day to get a quick practice session in. I’ve already had three friends buy or Order one within last two weeks after playing it or seeing drills available. Highly recommend!

The VR practice possible via the Meta Quest android platform is technically about as good as possible for the platform, and in many respects provides superior performance to other dry fire exercise methodologies.

While the subscription only approach of the simulation developer may be off putting to some, a subscription model helps to ensure long term developer existence and that program bugs/issues are addressed.

reviews.review_title: Best Training aid outside range time
reviews.review_description:
I’ve had my Ace for several weeks and already have 25,000+ rounds registered.
My speed and accuracy continues to climb. 
I’ve used the system to training officers at work and build skills for my kids. 
I can’t believe how convenient training can be.

I’ve really enjoyed using Ace. The variety of scenarios, training modules, and challenges keeps everything engaging, and the true competition-style stages make it feel realistic and practical. One of the biggest benefits is being able to get meaningful reps in without wasting ammo, while still sharpening real skills. My favorite features are the Taran Tactical and Pew View shooting ranges they’re immersive, well designed, and make practice both fun and effective. Overall, it’s a great app for anyone serious about improving performance in a smart, efficient way.

I am a hobby shooter, shooting Steel Challenge. My current classifications are B in both RFRO and RFPO. I was looking for a way to get more practice time. The range I shoot at is a 1 hour drive each way and the Steel Challenge plated are set up for 1 week every month (the other 3 weeks are USPSA, IDPA, and Cowboy Shooting). ACE allows me to practice daily and to try different approaches to each stage to see what works best for me. Although I've never shot any competitions besides Steel Challenge, I've been having a blast with the other disciplines and drills in ACE. I love the daily and weekly drills, and have found them very helpful. Also, the community has been fantastic. Whenever I can't quite figure out the best way to shoot something (that means you Mr. Polish plate rack!), I've always found helpful advice. Whether you're a new shooter or seasoned shooter, I feel ACE has a place in your shooting routine.

I absolutely love this app it seems expensive at first but I really wish I pulled the trigger on this years ago. I have only had this for 16 days but already put over 36k rounds down range actually improving speed and reaction times. I have also already recommended this to EVERYONE that likes gun even if the dont already have a VR it is that much fun, my wife even loves it and thats always a good thing!!!

I have US Army special operations experience and I bought a META Quest 3 exclusively to use the ACE Virtual Shooting app after hearing nothing but positive reviews from fellow veterans about it.

I haven’t been disappointed - makes it so easy for someone with a busy schedule like myself and without both easy and consistent access to a range to be able to not only maintain, but improve shooting skills with regularity.

Not to mention that I’ve saved over $11,000 worth of ammo expenditures in less than 4 months of using it.

Cannot recommend enough.  Has met every expectation and more.  One of my favorite investments in recent memory.

This is a great way to practice without hitting the range. It replicates different shooting scenarios for Practical Shooting courses and refining skills. There’s no recoil but that’s consistent with dry fire practice. Working on scores and times on different courses drives me to keep going. More than anything though it’s fun.

reviews.review_title: ACE is helping ME become an ACE
reviews.review_description:
I Love this!  Everyday is RANGE DAY in my NYC apt!  I practice everyday between breaks in a DISCIPLINED DAILY ROUTINE.

When I recently went to a FLORIDA range I was hitting Center Mass from 30 ft plus with MP SHIELD PLUS.   In the ACE SYSTEM the amount of options and daily challenges, are to much to remember!  Never bored.  A great tool to improve timing, sight, etc.  
I look forward to another hobby I love to do....Get Better! Thanks ACE!  Sincerely G ACE

I would give this program a -10 rating. 
The old program work this one does not

developer_response.body:
Hi,

I'm terribly sorry to hear you're having issues with our system. Please contact our support team at support@acexr.com to help us address whatever blockers you're running into!

reviews.review_title: Try new techniques and polish your skill
reviews.review_description: This is a great shooting simulator that has allowed me to try new grip techniques and really improve my draw and transition speeds.  The variety of drills and challenges keeps things interesting and fresh. This is a nice addition to dry firing, not a replacement.  If you’re a shooting enthusiast and want to improve your range game, this is a must have.

I was skeptical at first, but the app is actually great. Its helped me improve my times, and its easy to forget that its VR.  After a session of shooting it really feels like I just left the range. The Handsets they make are built really well, and have a accurate feeling weight to them. 

All in all, its a great system, and if you are on the fence about it its well worth it.

I’ve been using this for a little less than a month. It has helped me immensely with quickly acquiring my sight picture and with learning to move my sight picture more quickly and fluidly between targets.  The stages change fairly regularly and there’s enough of them to keep it interesting day after day. I have only shot one match since getting this, but it allowed me to increase my average stage score by 15%.  Almost all from running the stages faster.  Definitely worth the money!

reviews.review_title: If you compete, you need this.
reviews.review_description:
This is of course not a replacement for live fire or even dry fire with your actual firearm.   This however is great at training target transitions and stage planning.   Where else can you practice moving targets and Texas stars!   

My biggest complaint is red dot brightness. Maybe is a Quest 3s issue but the dot gets lost easily.

reviews.review_title: Never thought I would be a gamer!
reviews.review_description: The ACE shooting game is so much fun! I’m an old handgun shooter who never played game before. My kids got me this for my 65 th birthday and I’m having more fun with it than I ever imagined.

reviews.review_title: Best toll to skill up at home
reviews.review_description: As a young father, I really appreciated AceXR. It is definitely a convenient device, and at the same time very quiet. Now I can improve my skills without worry and simply practice in the evenings, which was often impossible with a real firearm because of my newborn sleeping just a few meters away. Hand on heart, I can truly recommend it.

I’ve been using Ace to train for a full season now, and it has played a major role in improving my Steel Challenge times while also helping me develop key IPSC skills.

Ace is an excellent complement to both dry fire and live-fire range training, making it easy to keep skills sharp and consistent. It’s a well-designed, practical, and effective app for anyone serious about improving their shooting performance.

reviews.review_title: Only reason I got the MQ.
reviews.review_description: This app and accessories was the only reason I got the MQ.  Don't get me wrong, I am having a ball with other apps too, but the Ace is the one app that I open every day and use.  Dry fire is so good for your shooting abilities and this makes it so fun!  In my research of this app and the MQ, my brother jumped onboard.  We live in different areas of the country but shoot in Ace several times a week now.  It's the coolest thing I have gotten in a long time.  Very impressed and having a ball.

Great for those days when the range isn’t available or weather is less than ideal. Can be used late at night or when the workday allows which is an absolute game changer. This has greatly improved my shooting in conjunction with more range time. Highly recommend.

I recently started getting serious about daily carry and bought me and my wife a daily carry. Being I work nights it's hard for me to go to the ranger all them time so I decided to give ACE a try. I ended up buying the quest 3 for the ACEVR and am very happy that I did. So far I have put more than 25k round through it. Not only is it a savings on ammo but it has helped me improve. I have my ups and down weeks but over all I have improved quite a bit. 

Now the part I do not like about it. I think it's absolutely ridiculous you can't buy a family pack or at least have 2 profiles. NEED TO ADD A FAMILY PACK OR AT THE VERY LEAST 2 PROFILES.

And my other issue I have with it is having to press A to end some of the stages. Once all targets have been hit the stage should end automatically. It gets to be a pain in the a$$ trying to find the A button when the controller is backwards in the gun. ALL STAGES SHOULD END AUTOMATICALLY WHEN ITS COMPLETE.

I’ve been using ACE VR everyday for almost a month as a supplemental training tool for practical shooting, and the improvement in my skills has been both measurable and noticeable. What initially felt like a simple dry-fire alternative has become a structured performance training platform.

Within a short period of consistent use, I’ve seen clear development in several core areas: draw efficiency, target transitions, visual discipline, and movement between positions. The platform has helped me understand concepts like overswing control, pacing transitions, and shot confirmation in a way that traditional dry fire alone did not. I’m not just getting repetitions — I’m building better mechanics and learning how to manage tension and efficiency under simulated stage conditions.

One of the biggest benefits has been how ACE allows me to isolate specific skills (like transitions or reloads), refine them with intent, and then immediately apply them in stage-style scenarios. That integration between drills and practical application mirrors real match environments and makes practice feel purposeful.

Beyond mechanics, ACE has improved my awareness. I can now identify when I’m rushing transitions, when tension creeps into my shoulders, and how pacing affects shot quality. That level of feedback accelerates learning in a way that static practice cannot.

Overall, ACE has been an effective and engaging training tool that supports real-world competition improvement. It has helped me build consistency, confidence, and a clearer understanding of performance fundamentals — and I look forward to continuing to develop with it.

Thank you!

Received the Ace setup as a Christmas gift and it has become part of my daily routine. Love the ability to run stages instead of dry firing at light switches. Seems to have good update support and new stages have come across a couple times since I’ve had it. Excited to see what’s to come with this system.
I was very intrigued in this game so I bought and wow it’s addicting. I can definitely see myself improving already and the drills are fun and challenging. Would 100% recommend

I was a combat engineer for 11 years in the army and had a decent amount of pistol training. I took 3 months off from shooting and only played this game with the staccato p controller every day for roughly 1000 rounds per day. After that I went to the range and shot my sister's mc9 pro, it was very obvious I had improved a great deal. My 10 yard grouping on bill drills was tighter, the optic reset and split time all improved. I highly recommend this to anyone that even kind of likes firearms.

reviews.review_title: Closest to going to a range
reviews.review_description: By far the best dry fire system I've ever used. I've tried other products and nothing comes close to this. Very impressive handset and the program is so realistic. Sometimes there's small issues and glitches but not a real problem that I've seen. Matched with the ACE handset and it's a game changer for building skills that translate to real shooting.

AceXR is an outstanding training platform that blends realism with accessibility in a way that makes consistent practice both effective and genuinely enjoyable. The stage design, performance metrics, and structured drills provide meaningful feedback that translates well to real-world fundamentals. Whether working transitions, draw speed, or stage planning, the software delivers repeatable reps without the constraints of time, weather, or ammunition cost. At the same time, it’s simply a blast to use — competitive, immersive, and engaging. For anyone serious about improving while keeping practice fun, AceXR truly feels like an Ace up your sleeve.
Awesome trainer to save money on rounds. Tons of stages and series to practice with. Everything is realistic. Doesn’t mimic actual recoil/feedback but great in addition to live fire and dry firing

reviews.review_title: Best kept secret in firearms training
reviews.review_description: This is a game changer in firearms training.  After trying for the first time I shortly after bought my own meta quest and ACE pistol.  Goal was to shoot 3x per week, got addicted and became where I’d be upset if I missed one day during the week. Huge improvements in live fire testing in a very short amount of time using ACE VR.  Would absolutely recommend to anyone looking to advance their firearms training and skill level. Extra bonus is the $$ savings from live fire practice.  Five stars but if there was six, I would give six stars.

My friend let me try their Ace setup and I was instantly hooked. I already had a quest 2 so I bought my own Ace handset and have since shot over 110k rounds. I’ve made it a daily habit to shoot on ace and a few other friends have also invested and got their own. It’s a ton of fun to get on with friends and shoot together. You aren’t able to train recoil management but the ability to acquire your dot, transition targets, and process information have been valuable tools that will translate to live fire.

I love this app! It is a great companion to live fire and dry fire. I really appreciate the ability to work on visually tracking the dot through transitions, that has always been a spot I needed a little work on. It is very easy, fun and economical to practice this in the app, and it shows during my live fire training.

As a law enforcement officer and firearms instructor I have found that this is one of the best training tools available. It has helped me work on specific points and fundamentals of firearms. The open ranges are perfect and the drills are fun and challenging. I had also been impressed with the amount of updates and new content added. Highly recommend this.

This game is the #1 reason to purchase a Quest 3. The device itself has alot of utility and games but if Ace was the ONLY thing it did I'd still buy one. I would have bought one sooner if I knew how much I was gonna like it.

reviews.review_title: Awesome Training and a lot of fun
reviews.review_description: I've looked at this system for over a year and finally "pulled the trigger" after talking with a buddy who just hit GM who loves his. I wanted it for a training device more than a "game and I have not been disappointed. I'm a 30yr LEO, 18 yr FFL/SOT, 30yr private Firearms Instructor, as well as an avid competition shooter in USPSA, IDPA, and 3 gun. I haven't had the AceXR that long but I am now pushing past 50k rounds. This is the best training device I have found. I am the range master for my for my agency on open range days. I took the ACE VR OUT and ran several scenarios in the program that I could set up and reproduce in real time. I found that my accuracy and speed were only about .15-.25 different between the two. Now I am shooting a full on race gun that is almost identical to my handset and I shoot hand loaded ammo  so my gun in very flat shooting and if you shoot a, sat, P365 you are going to feel the difference a bit more bit the sight picture, sight alignment, target acquisition is still very much on par with the real thing. You can game it and cut corners if you want and are concerned with the highest possible scorebut I want if for the training and the full experience. The shoot don't shoot as well at the pieing the door an others are great cheap LEO instruction. My son and I both shot the 25 handgun nationals through the game outside on our property and had an absolute blast. I'm so looking forward to where ace takes this in the near future. PCC, 3 gun? Full on police training scenarios? I am 1000% pleased and happy with my purchase. The simulator is very detailed an accurate and it just works well.

Using this system has less to a great boost in my skill level while adding some fun and competitiveness into my dry fire training. This is a great tool for shooters if all levels whether you just shoot recreationally or you are a high level competition shooter.
